releases.title: Local model insights: energy & cloud savings

highlights.title: Energy consumption insights
highlights.description: TokenTelemetry now estimates the energy consumed (in Watt-hours) for local generation based on your actual hardware's power configuration and model throughput.

highlights.title: Savings vs. Cloud estimates
highlights.description: Ever wonder how much you're saving by running models locally? TokenTelemetry compares your local generation cost against a configurable reference cloud model (defaulting to Claude 3.5 Sonnet) so you can track your API savings.

releases.title: Real cost for the local models you run

highlights.title: Local models priced by your actual hardware
highlights.description: Sessions on Ollama, llama.cpp or vLLM are now detected by their endpoint/provider — not by guessing — so a local model is always costed by electricity, never at cloud API rates (even when it shares a name with a cloud model). Generation time uses each model's real throughput where measurable (a 4B isn't costed like a 70B).

highlights.title: Set or measure your machine's power
highlights.description: A new Settings → Local power & electricity card lets you set your watts-under-load and kWh rate, mark local/LAN and flat-subscription endpoints, and — where your OS allows it without admin — measure real power draw directly. The estimate is honest about where its numbers come from.

highlights.title: 2000+ models priced for accurate cost estimates
highlights.description: Cost estimates now draw on a much larger, regularly-refreshed pricing dataset (sourced from models.dev), so far more models — and provider-specific rates — get an accurate price instead of a generic fallback. The data ships with each update and is read entirely on your machine: no new outbound calls, same local-first privacy.

highlights.title: Local models priced by electricity, not fake API rates
highlights.description: Models you run yourself (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM) have no API bill — their real cost is power. Set your machine's watts under load and your kWh rate, and TokenTelemetry estimates the electricity cost instead of inventing a per-token charge.

highlights.title: Flat subscriptions now show $0 per call
highlights.description: Endpoints you pay for monthly (Ollama Cloud Pro, a proxied gateway) are billed by subscription, not per token. List them as subscription endpoints and their sessions stop inflating your per-call cost totals.

highlights.title: Full subscription-cost disclaimer
highlights.description: The dashboard's "API equiv. (est.)" figure used to cut off mid-sentence at "Subscription users p…". The disclaimer now shows in full, plus a persistent callout makes clear the number re-prices your usage at API list rates for comparing sessions — it is not an invoice. On a flat-fee plan like Claude Pro/Max or Copilot, your actual spend is much lower.

highlights.title: Per-agent billing mode — bill vs. estimate, framed right
highlights.description: If you pay per token (API key) for an agent, the cost figure approximates your real bill; on a flat subscription it's only an API-list-price equivalent. TokenTelemetry now auto-detects which one you're on per agent (from each agent's own auth — e.g. ~/.codex/auth.json) and frames the number accordingly, with a Settings → Billing & cost control to override any agent.

releases.title: Update check — now visible and yours to control

highlights.title: Check-for-updates toggle
highlights.description: TokenTelemetry's one outbound call — an optional check that tells you when new features ship — now has a switch in Settings → Updates & privacy. It sends no usage data; turn it off any time, or set TT_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1 to enforce it off.

highlights.title: Clearer privacy wording
highlights.description: Docs and site now state precisely what stays local (all your logs, sessions, tokens, and costs) and what the update check does, so the privacy promise matches the behavior.

releases.title: Antigravity CLI — real model & accurate projects

highlights.title: Real model for Antigravity CLI sessions
highlights.description: Sessions from the Antigravity CLI (agy) now show the actual model they ran (e.g. "Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)") instead of a generic "gemini (antigravity)" label, read straight from each session's own trajectory.

highlights.title: Accurate, stable project for Antigravity CLI sessions
highlights.description: CLI sessions are now grouped under the exact folder they worked in, read from each session's own saved record — so the grouping stays correct over time instead of guessing from task text. Sessions that genuinely never entered a project (pure chat/research) stay grouped separately, off the Projects view.

highlights.title: OpenAI-compatible summarizer backend
highlights.description: Point TokenTelemetry at any server speaking the OpenAI /v1/chat/completions API — llama.cpp, vLLM, LM Studio, LocalAI, or Ollama's OpenAI endpoint — to generate trace summaries. No extra CLI to install: set the endpoint, model, and (optionally) an API key, tune sampling, and hit Test connection.

releases.title: Antigravity — tokens, IDE coverage, surface labels

highlights.title: Antigravity token usage
highlights.description: Antigravity sessions now show estimated token usage instead of zero, read from the session transcripts.

highlights.title: Antigravity IDE sessions tracked
highlights.description: Sessions from the Antigravity IDE (not just the CLI) are now picked up, and overlapping sessions are de-duplicated so counts stay accurate.

highlights.title: Antigravity surface sub-label
highlights.description: Antigravity sessions carry a small CLI / IDE / APP tag so you can tell which surface a session came from. They still roll up under one Antigravity total.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot CLI sessions tracked
highlights.description: TokenTelemetry now reads GitHub Copilot CLI / agent sessions from ~/.copilot/session-state, so your terminal Copilot usage shows up alongside everything else — model, tokens, project, and full trace.

highlights.title: Copilot surface sub-label
highlights.description: Copilot sessions now carry a small CLI or VS Code tag so you can tell at a glance which surface a session came from. They still roll up under one Copilot total.

highlights.title: Grok Build (xAI) support
highlights.description: TokenTelemetry now tracks xAI's Grok Build coding agent — sessions, context usage, tool calls, permission decisions, and plan mode — with a dedicated forensics card. That's 11 agents tracked in total.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot tracking restored
highlights.description: Recent VS Code versions changed how Copilot chats are stored on disk, which made new sessions read as zero. TokenTelemetry now reads the new format, so your recent Copilot sessions show up again.

highlights.title: Smarter 'Back' from a session
highlights.description: Opening a session and hitting Back now returns you to the page you came from — e.g. the project you were viewing — instead of always jumping to the dashboard.

highlights.title: Summarizer settings
highlights.description: Pick a backend (Ollama / Codex / Claude / Gemini / Qwen / Antigravity) and choose a specific model for it. Avoids 'incorrect API key' errors when your account doesn't have GPT-5, or 'timed out' when Ollama's default model is too big for your hardware.

highlights.title: Configurable ports
highlights.description: start.sh now accepts --port (frontend) and --api-port (backend), e.g. start.sh --port 4000 --api-port 9000. The frontend is told the backend's location automatically via NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE, so fetches always reach the right place.

highlights.title: Hermes Agent dashboard
highlights.description: New sub-pages for Gateway, Memory, Profiles, Schedules, Skills, Soul, and Tools — all read directly from your local ~/.hermes state.
